description: Healthcare IT, clinical AI, and medical informatics concepts for technical interviews

topics.explanation: Electronic Medical Records (EMR) are digital versions of a patient's chart within a single practice — not easily shared externally. Electronic Health Records (EHR) are interoperable across providers and systems, supporting care coordination. HIPAA and HL7 FHIR standards govern data exchange.
sample_questions: What is the key difference between an EMR and an EHR?
What role does HL7 FHIR play in healthcare data exchange?

topics.explanation: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) requires protection of Protected Health Information (PHI). Technical safeguards: encryption at rest and in transit, access controls, audit logs. Business Associate Agreements (BAA) required for third-party vendors handling PHI.
sample_questions: What technical safeguards does HIPAA require?
When is a Business Associate Agreement required?

topics.explanation: ICD-10 (International Classification of Diseases) codes are used for diagnosis documentation and insurance billing. ICD-10-CM (clinical modification) has ~70,000 codes. Accurate coding affects reimbursement, clinical research, and epidemiological tracking. NLP can automate coding from clinical notes.
sample_questions: What is ICD-10 and why is it important in healthcare IT?
How can NLP assist with medical coding from clinical notes?

topics.explanation: CDSS tools assist clinicians with drug interaction checks, dosing alerts, diagnostic suggestions, and guideline adherence. Alert fatigue (too many low-value alerts) is a major challenge. Rule-based systems are transparent; ML-based systems are powerful but require explainability (SHAP, LIME) for clinical trust.
sample_questions: What is alert fatigue and how would you reduce it in a CDSS?
Why is explainability critical in clinical AI?

topics.concept: Symptom Checker AI — Design and Liability
topics.explanation: AI symptom checkers perform triage, not diagnosis. Key design principles: confidence thresholds, mandatory escalation paths, human-in-the-loop review for high-risk outputs, regulatory compliance (FDA SaMD rules). Liability risk is managed by positioning the tool as decision support, not autonomous diagnosis.
sample_questions: How would you design a symptom checker AI while managing clinical liability?
What does FDA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulation require?

topics.explanation: Applying deep learning (CNNs) to medical imaging faces: class imbalance (rare diseases), annotation quality (requires radiologist labels), domain shift (different scanner manufacturers), interpretability requirements (saliency maps, Grad-CAM), and FDA 510(k) clearance for diagnostic use.
sample_questions: What are the main challenges of applying ML to medical imaging?
How do you address class imbalance in a radiology AI dataset?

topics.concept: Natural Language Processing in Clinical Notes
topics.explanation: Clinical NLP extracts structured data from free-text notes: named entity recognition for conditions/medications, relation extraction, negation detection ('no fever'). Models: BioBERT, ClinicalBERT, scispaCy. Challenges: medical abbreviations, negation, misspellings, PHI de-identification.
sample_questions: How does negation detection differ in clinical NLP versus general NLP?
What is BioBERT and why is it preferred over standard BERT for medical text?

topics.explanation: Federated learning trains models across hospital nodes without sharing raw patient data — only model gradients are aggregated. Addresses privacy regulations (HIPAA, GDPR). Challenges: non-IID data distributions across hospitals, communication efficiency, gradient poisoning attacks. Differential privacy adds formal privacy guarantees.
sample_questions: How does federated learning preserve patient privacy while training a shared model?
What is the non-IID data challenge in federated learning for healthcare?

topics.explanation: FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) is a modern API standard for healthcare data exchange using REST and JSON/XML. Resources represent clinical entities (Patient, Observation, Medication). SMART on FHIR adds OAuth2-based authorisation for third-party apps accessing EHR data.
sample_questions: What makes FHIR better than older HL7 v2 for modern healthcare APIs?
What is SMART on FHIR used for?

topics.explanation: DDI detection uses knowledge graphs (DrugBank, NDF-RT), rule-based engines, and ML classifiers on structured prescriptions. NLP can also extract DDI mentions from literature. Real-time checks at the point of prescribing require sub-second latency. Severity levels (contraindicated, major, moderate, minor) drive alert thresholds.
sample_questions: How would you build a real-time drug-drug interaction checker?
How do you represent drug knowledge for machine reasoning?
